//: === Agent mode example — fully autonomous single-LLM runtime ===
// purpose: Minimal example showing the shape of an agent config. Copy and edit for your own mission. Unlike pipeline configs, agent configs have no phases — the agent decides what to work on.

harness.// max_tokens: Leave headroom below the model's ceiling; 16K is safe for Sonnet-class models.
harness.// api_key: Set via HARNESS_API_KEY env var in production; leave empty in the tracked example.
harness.// max_tool_turns: Agent cycles can be long (read + plan + edit + verify per cycle). 200 is a reasonable cap; Stage B's per-turn parallel execution keeps wall-clock tolerable even at this depth.

mission: You are the maintainer of this codebase. Each cycle, pick the single most valuable improvement you can make right now — a real bug, a dead code path to prune, a test that's missing, a rough API to polish. Prefer small coherent changes to big rewrites. End the mission only when you believe the codebase is in materially better shape than when you started; otherwise keep iterating.
